title: RAG Domain Definition
description: Schema for validating domain YAML files used by the Generic Multi-Domain RAG platform. Each YAML file defines one domain with its document types, metadata extraction rules, classification logic, guardrails, and prompt templates.

id.description: Unique domain identifier. Lowercase, alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores. Used as the partition key in metadata and as the {domainId} path variable in the REST API.

display-name.description: Human-readable domain name shown in the UI and API responses.

enabled.description: Whether this domain is active. Disabled domains are not loaded into the DomainRegistry and their API endpoints return 404.

documents-path.description: Filesystem path to the folder containing documents for batch ingestion. Supports Spring-style ${ENV_VAR:default} placeholders.

items.description: File extension including the leading dot.

supported-file-types.description: File extensions accepted for ingestion in this domain.

chunk-size.description: Maximum number of tokens per text segment when splitting documents.

chunk-overlap.description: Number of overlapping tokens between consecutive segments.

entity-id-key.description: The domain-specific entity identifier key (e.g. candidate_id, matter_id, patient_id). Stored in the shared base metadata field 'entity_id'.

stop-words.description: Domain-specific words filtered out during query term extraction. These are words that appear in every document within this domain and carry no discriminating value.

extraction.description: Model ID used for LLM-based metadata extraction in this domain. References a model defined in application.yml app.models.definitions. Individual fields can override this with their own 'model' key.

query.description: Model ID used for LLM answer generation during queries. Typically a more capable model than the extraction model.

models.description: Optional model configuration per purpose. Model IDs reference entries in application.yml app.models.definitions.

classification-rules.description: Rules evaluated top-down by priority (highest first) to classify a document's doc_type. First match wins.

doc-types.description: Map of doc_type identifiers to their definitions. Each key must match a doc-type value referenced in classification-rules.

guardrails.description: Safety rules evaluated before every query. First blocking rule wins.

prompts.description: Prompt templates used for LLM answer generation.

doc-type.description: The doc_type identifier assigned when this rule matches. Must correspond to a key in the doc-types map.

priority.description: Higher values are evaluated first. Use low priority (e.g. 1) for fallback/default rules.

filename-patterns.description: Glob patterns matched against the filename. A match on any pattern satisfies the filename condition.

content-keywords.description: Keywords searched in the document text (case-insensitive). The number of hits is compared against min-keyword-hits.

min-keyword-hits.description: Minimum number of content-keywords that must appear in the text for this rule to match. Set to 0 for unconditional match (useful for fallback rules).

display-name.description: Human-readable name for this document type.

metadata.description: Ordered list of metadata fields to extract from documents of this type.

key.description: Metadata key stored in the embedding segment. Must be unique within a doc_type.

type.description: Data type of the extracted value.

extraction.description: Extraction strategy used to populate this field.

model.description: Optional model ID override for this specific field. When set, overrides domain.models.extraction for this field only. References a model defined in application.yml app.models.definitions. Only meaningful when extraction is 'llm' or when a composite strategy includes an 'llm' sub-strategy.

prompt.description: LLM prompt sent along with the document text. Required when extraction is 'llm'. The document text is appended automatically by the engine.

patterns.description: Regex patterns tried in order; first capture group match is returned. Required when extraction is 'regex'.

keywords.description: Map of category label → keyword list. Text is scanned for keywords; the first matching category is returned. Required when extraction is 'keyword'.

strategies.description: Ordered list of sub-strategies. Tried in sequence; first non-null result wins. Required when extraction is 'composite'.

type.description: The sub-strategy type. Cannot be 'composite' (no recursive nesting).

model.description: Optional model ID override for this LLM sub-strategy within a composite.

prompt.description: LLM prompt. Required when type is 'llm'.

patterns.description: Regex patterns. Required when type is 'regex'.

keywords.description: Keyword map. Required when type is 'keyword'.

rules.description: Ordered list of guardrail rules. Evaluated in sequence; first blocking rule stops evaluation and returns its blocked-message.

type.description: Guardrail rule type. 'term-block' matches trigger-terms + optional intent-terms. 'pattern-block' matches regex patterns. 'llm-block' delegates the safety decision to an LLM.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this rule protects against.

model.description: Model ID for llm-block rules. References a model defined in application.yml app.models.definitions. If omitted, falls back to app.models.default-model.

prompt.description: System prompt for llm-block rules. Defines the safety policy the LLM should enforce. Should include few-shot examples of BLOCK and PASS decisions. Required when type is 'llm-block'.

trigger-terms.description: Terms that trigger this rule (case-insensitive word boundary match). Required when type is 'term-block'.

intent-terms.description: Intent terms that must also be present when require-both is true. Used with type 'term-block'.

require-both.description: If true, both a trigger-term AND an intent-term must match for the rule to block. If false, trigger-terms alone are sufficient. Default: false.

patterns.description: Regex patterns matched against the query text (case-insensitive). Any match triggers the block. Required when type is 'pattern-block'.

blocked-message.description: Message returned to the user when this guardrail triggers.

query.description: System prompt template for LLM answer generation. Must contain exactly two '%s' placeholders: first for context (retrieved segments), second for the user question.

fallback.description: Template used when the LLM is unavailable. Must contain one '%d' placeholder for the number of matched excerpts.
